title: eleocharis tetraquetra f. tsurumachii
intro: Eleocharis tetraquetra f. tsurumachii, also known as Japanese spike rush, is a plant species belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Japan, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Japanese spike rush include its potential to aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to promote wound healing and reduce swelling. Japanese spike rush can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in traditional Japanese medicine to make a topical ointment for skin and wound care.
